title: Chapter 8 - Good Habits: Clean History and Clear Messages
description: Check your understanding of good Git habits and VS Code workflow tips.

options.label: i did some stuff at school i forgor

explanations.overview: Good commit messages are short but specific, describing what changed.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. This message is vague and unprofessional.
byOptionId.b: Correct. It describes a specific change clearly.
byOptionId.c: Not quite. Bread is not a helpful commit message.
byOptionId.d: Not quite. Personal preferences are not helpful in history.

questions.prompt: Why are small, focused commits helpful?

options.label: They make your keyboard type faster
They are easier to review and easier to undo

explanations.overview: Focused commits keep history readable and make troubleshooting easier.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Commits do not affect typing speed.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Conflicts can still happen, but good commits can make them easier to resolve.
byOptionId.c: Correct. Small commits help with review, debugging, and undoing changes.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Pull requests are created on GitHub.

questions.prompt: What is a README.md commonly used for?

options.label: Explaining what the project is and how to use it

explanations.overview: READMEs document a project for users and future contributors.

byOptionId.a: Correct. A README explains what the repo is and how to work with it.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Secrets should not be committed; use environment variables instead.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. A README is documentation, not the code itself.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Branch cleanup is a Git/GitHub workflow, not the job of README.

questions.prompt: What is a Git tag best described as?

options.label: A branch that always has conflicts
A named bookmark pointing to a specific commit

explanations.overview: Tags mark important points in history, like a release version.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. Hiding changes is more like stashing or ignoring.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Tags are not staging tools.
byOptionId.d: Correct. Tags point to commits as named markers.
